programmes.show_description: Paramedics treat a patient with an unusual stomach condition and a 10-year-old girl who's having an asthma attack and isn't responding to her usual medication.
Staff nurse Sophie has only been at King's for three weeks. One of her patients is James, a chef who accidentally stabbed himself in the thigh while chopping tomatoes.
A look at the bond between parents and children, including the youngest patient yet to be featured on the series, and what happens when the roles are reversed due to illness.
Five Brits compete to see who hosts the best holiday. In Benidorm, singer Wyen splashes out on a luxury yacht and, ever the centre of attention, performs a self-penned song. (Ep1/20)
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Bristol. Big kid and party girl Jade kicks off the week with a festival-themed evening, complete with a drum and bass rave. Will it be a crowd-pleasing smash or a total washout? (Ep1/5)
Bristol. Footy fan Adam dedicates his dinner to his favourite sport. But will an evening of soccer splendour impress his guests or leave them calling foul when a red card's shown? (Ep2/5)
Bristol. Property developer Joe treats his guests to a Caribbean-themed evening dedicated to the happiness of holidaying. But will they feel like they're in paradise or purgatory? (Ep3/5)
Bristol. Jazz band leader Jack hosts a night of vintage couture, sophistication and class. But when his timings and temperatures pile on the pressure, can he rise above the chaos? (Ep4/5)
Bristol. Nanny Sarah serves up the last meal of the week, and it's sophisticated... children's food. Can she delight her diners or will they throw their toys out the pram? (Ep5/5)
Steve and Helen have travelled the world in search of the perfect holiday home and have settled on Torrox on the Costa del Sol. Can Scarlette Douglas find the right property for their £95k?
Tony and Hendrik hunt for their dream holiday home on Portugal's Setúbal Peninsula, for £330k. Can Danni Menzies find the perfect place for all their family to enjoy?
Dance teacher Karen has sold up in the UK and is after a permanent place in Charente for £210k. Will any of Jean Johansson's options make Karen feel footloose?
When Yvonne and Tony first visited Cyprus they immediately fell in love. Laura Hamilton helps them find a home in the stunning coastal town of Paralimni for £80k.
Nick and Rose want a holiday home on the Caribbean island of Barbados, to spend up to six months a year enjoying the climate and golf facilities. Craig Rowe shows them what £550k can buy.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer meet a Preston couple torn between adding a luxury loft conversion to their three-bed semi or buying a stunning new home nearby.
In York, Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help Tom and Timna find their first family home. And Rich and Kirsty want a house they can grow into after living it up in London.
Helen and her daughter Lauren begin the competition at North Kingsfield Holiday Cottages in Yorkshire. But the bathrooms are showered with criticism and the lack of mirrors reflects badly.
At Crackin' View Guest House in Northumberland, Steve and Maria reckon their rivals will be blown away by their... cracking view. But there's a bangers clanger at breakfast.
Ali and his pal Serge host the third visit of the week, at the Domo Boutique Hotel in Birmingham. Helen's dazzled by the décor, but trouble brews over a caffeine conundrum.
At The Crown Inn in Norfolk, Lisa and Neil hope that their historic setting will bag them the B&B crown. But there are 'paneful' observations about the windows and a lorry-load of noise.
On the final day, the B&Bers discover how much they've been paid. Comments about curtains and off-colour feedback about bedding leave some hosts open-mouthed, before the winner is crowned.
Brand new series: A patient experiences an unstoppable nosebleed. Paramedics diagnose a heart attack. And a drunk man who's collapsed in the street refuses medical help.
Joan, who's 87, comes to Queen's in Nottingham after falling on a tram and cutting her head open. Rose, who's 19, faces life-changing injuries after being hit by a car.
Young Henry has been hit by a forklift truck driven by his dad. Best mates Dean and Nahum are reeling from a car crash. And 94-year-old Pearl shares a tale of wartime romance.
Paramedics treat a man in severe pain who has an umbilical hernia, and are called out to a mum who has just delivered her baby on the bathroom floor.
Joan, who's 87, comes to Queen's in Nottingham after falling on a tram and cutting her head open. Rose, who's 19, faces life-changing injuries after being hit by a car.
Young Henry has been hit by a forklift truck driven by his dad. Best mates Dean and Nahum are reeling from a car crash. And 94-year-old Pearl shares a tale of wartime romance.
Five Brits compete to see who hosts the best holiday. On day two in Benidorm, Lisa takes the group quad biking, but fails to keep everyone on track. (Ep2/20)
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Claire Hilton welcomes her guests to Robertsbrook Guesthouse in Swanage, where a dust hunt yields some undesired results, and talk of star ratings causes tension.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Simon O'Brien leads Henry Cole to a barn with an amazing collection of replica Rolls-Royce cars, and Henry tries his hand at driving a tractor.
Chris and Marie are retiring to Manilva on Spain's Costa del Sol. Chris wants a place on a golf resort, but Marie would prefer to live closer to town. Can Danni Menzies find a compromise?
Nottingham and Derby. American Hali serves a travel-inspired menu, including Moroccan chicken cigars and an unusual dessert, on a fun-filled night of impressions and dodgy dancing. (Ep1/5)
Nottingham and Derby. Alistair hopes to step the competition up a gear with a risqué menu including prunes and a chilli soup dessert. But he's on thin ice with Lesley. (Ep2/5)
Nottingham and Derby. Funeral arranger Kim offers a mixed bag menu and games galore, on an evening featuring geriatric jibes and a dance-off. (Ep3/5)
Nottingham and Derby. Customer relations associate and part-time drag queen Adam plans a theatrical night of flamboyance. What will his guests make of his secret hobby? (Ep4/5)
Nottingham and Derby. The last host, Lesley, plans a glitzy, glamorous evening, with an awards ceremony and tiaras. But there are shocks and tears, and the main doesn't go to plan. (Ep5/5)
The competition kicks off at My Way Guest House in County Durham. Hosts Kaye and Lily pride themselves on cleanliness, so it's a shock when two guests make some dusty discoveries.
David and Sophie hope that their award-winning brekkie at The Old Orleton Inn in Shropshire will win them the week. But the portion sizes lead to some stomach-rumbling grumbles.
Amanda and Trudy host at the pet-friendly Ambles House in Northampton. The homely hospitality makes everyone feel like part of the family, but there's an uninvited guest at breakfast.
Richard and Harriet pride themselves on their high-end rooms at The Royal George in Tintern, Monmouthshire. Their guests lap up the luxury but some less-than-perfect scores shock the hosts.
It's the final day and the B&Bers meet one last time to settle some scores. After bathroom blues and cleanliness concerns are aired, the payments are revealed and the winner is announced.
Golf GTI Mk2: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend fix up a VW Golf GTI Mk2 - an 80s classic that belongs to former bus driver Darren, who was tragically injured in a road accident.
Triumph TR7: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend make Barry's automotive dream come true by transforming his 1980 Triumph TR7 into a stunning, full-blown race car.
Kelpie Tess could be the perfect training companion for eight-year-old Sophie. And Lolla might be the right big, goofy dog that tai chi instructor Amanda needs.
Dan and Nina want to build a unique family home, but their plot is dominated by a pond filled with local drainage. Will their West Sussex house be sublime and sleek or a Shrek-like swamp?
...Clive and Kids: Spring is in the air and the family juggle lambing season with making the renovation watertight. And there's exciting news from the planners... (Ep4)
This episode looks at how life can change in the blink of an eye. A woman fights for her life after a shocking random act of violence. And Tom, who's 12, has been hit by a car.
Grandmother Rose, who's 80, has fluid on her lungs and is having trouble breathing, and 55-year-old trucker Kevin has serious injuries after his lorry rolled down a verge.
